questions.q: Explain the importance of warm-up exercises in sports training. How does it affect an athlete's performance during competition? (5 marks)
questions.answer: Warm-up exercises are essential in sports training as they prepare the body for physical activity, reducing the risk of injury and improving performance. A well-structured warm-up increases blood flow, heart rate, and temperature, making muscles more flexible and ready for intense exercise. It also enhances neuromuscular coordination, allowing athletes to perform at their best during competition.
marking_scheme: Understanding the importance of warm-up exercises (1 mark)
Explaining its effect on performance (1 mark)

questions.q: A basketball player needs to cover a distance of 20 meters in 4 seconds. What is the average speed of the player in meters per second? (5 marks)
questions.answer: Average speed = Distance / Time = 20 m / 4 s = 5 m/s
marking_scheme: Correct application of the formula (1 mark)
questions.topic: Speed, Distance, and Time in Sports

questions.q: Discuss the role of cool-down exercises in sports training. How does it help in preventing injuries and reducing muscle soreness? (5 marks)
questions.answer: Cool-down exercises are an essential part of sports training, helping to prevent injuries and reduce muscle soreness. After intense exercise, cool-down exercises gradually bring the body back to its resting state, reducing heart rate and blood pressure. This helps to prevent dizziness and fainting, while also reducing muscle soreness and inflammation.
marking_scheme: Understanding the purpose of cool-down exercises (1 mark)
Explaining its effect on preventing injuries (1 mark)
Describing its impact on muscle soreness (1 mark)

questions.q: A football player runs at an average speed of 5 meters per second for 15 seconds. What distance does the player cover in this time? (5 marks)
questions.answer: Distance = Speed * Time = 5 m/s * 15 s = 75 m
marking_scheme: Correct application of the formula (1 mark)
questions.topic: Speed, Distance, and Time in Sports

questions.q: Explain the concept of interval training in sports. How does it help in improving cardiovascular endurance? (5 marks)
questions.answer: Interval training is a type of training where athletes alternate between periods of high-intensity exercise and periods of low-intensity exercise or rest. This type of training helps to improve cardiovascular endurance by increasing the body's ability to transport oxygen and nutrients to the muscles during intense exercise.
marking_scheme: Understanding the concept of interval training (1 mark)
Explaining its effect on cardiovascular endurance (1 mark)

questions.q: A cyclist covers a distance of 30 kilometers in 45 minutes. What is the average speed of the cyclist in kilometers per hour? (5 marks)
questions.answer: Average speed = Distance / Time = 30 km / (45/60) h = 40 km/h
marking_scheme: Correct application of the formula (1 mark)
questions.topic: Speed, Distance, and Time in Sports
